chunks.text: EECS alumni Dawn Tilbury (EE PhD '94, advisor: Shankar Sastry) and Feng Zhou (CS PhD '07, advisor: Eric Brewer) have been selected to present keynote addresses at the Berkeley China Summit (BCS) 2020 conference, which will be held virtually on September 18-19th. BCS is a full-day conference that aims to foster collaboration and innovation between Berkeley and China in technology and entrepreneurship.
chunks.question: Who are the keynote speakers at BCS 2020?

chunks.text: The creators of BerkeleyDB (BDB) have won the 2020 Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) Special Interest Group on Management of Data (SIGMOD) Systems Award for their seminal work in embodying simplicity, quality, and elegance in a high-performance key-value store that has impacted many systems and applications over the last 25 years.
chunks.question: What award did BerkeleyDB win in 2020?

chunks.text: A paper co-written by EECS alumnus Peter Mattis (B.S. '97) is being presented at the 2020 ACM SIGMOD International Conference on Management of Data this month. The paper, titled 'CockroachDB: The Resilient Geo-Distributed SQL Database,' describes a cloud-native, distributed SQL database designed for resilience and scalability in global deployments.
chunks.question: What is the title of Peter Mattis's paper?
chunks.answer: CockroachDB: The Resilient Geo-Distributed SQL Database.

chunks.text: CS major Jason (XiangJun) Li and a few friends have developed a website called LiveCoronaUpdates.org to provide clear, reliable, up-to-date numbers and trends on the COVID-19 outbreak for average Americans, particularly those on mobile phones. The site uses data from the World Health Organization and official government sources.
chunks.question: Who created the Coronavirus Tracker website?

chunks.text: Pieter Abbeel, co-founder, president and chief scientist of Covariant, is featured in articles in major publications like The New York Times and Wall Street Journal about robots enabled by Covariant's technology going live, highlighting advancements in robotics and AI applications in industry.
chunks.question: What company is Pieter Abbeel associated with?

chunks.text: RISC-V, a royalty-free microprocessor architecture first developed at Berkeley, is emerging as a rival to Arm, the most successful microchip architecture globally. The first RISC-V chip was built in 2011 as part of the open source Peer Lab Project by CS Prof. Krste Asanović, showcasing its origins and potential impact on the semiconductor industry.
chunks.question: What is RISC-V?

chunks.text: BLUE (Berkeley robot for Learning in Unstructured Environments), developed by EECS Prof. Pieter Abbeel and Project Blue, is named one of Popular Science's 'Best of What's New' innovations for 2019. It uses AI and deep reinforcement learning to operate safely in unpredictable settings, such as homes and workplaces.
chunks.question: What award did robot BLUE receive?
chunks.answer: Popular Science's Best of What's New.

chunks.text: EECS Prof. Dawn Song (Ph.D. '02) has been selected as a 2019 Fellow of the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM), cited for contributions to security and privacy. This honor places her among less than 1% of ACM's global membership, recognizing her expertise in computer security and trustworthy AI.
chunks.question: Why was Dawn Song named an ACM Fellow?
chunks.answer: For contributions to security and privacy.

chunks.text: EECS Prof. Trevor Darrell has been appointed chief scientist at Grabango, a provider of checkout-free technology for brick-and-mortar stores. An expert in computer vision and machine learning, he leads the Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Laboratory and contributed to CAFFE, a deep learning framework.
chunks.question: What is Trevor Darrell's new role?

chunks.text: CS alumna and Prof. Dawn Song is featured in a New York Times article titled 'Building a World Where Data Privacy Exists Online.' As a leading expert in computer security and AI, she founded Oasis Labs to develop technologies that enhance data privacy and security in online environments.
chunks.question: What startup did Dawn Song found?
